Always bit of a dilemma: 'use a producode constant in a test assert'

I think my preference would be to use string literal in the assert... (SQ wont complain becasu ethis is not a .java file :-))
I see now they were srting literals, as argued above, I think that was correct, please restore
I dont think this is used/needed

no need to return DataNode with realistic data that is not used by the test, just an example ID is enough even that is strictly not needed. All the test is proving is that the SAME collection from the service is returned.


usually these tets should look something like this

given: 'the service returns a collection of elements
   myDummyCollecion = [new someElement()]
   myMock.doSomething(withExactExpectedParameters) >> someSampleCollection
when: 'my I do somethign ' (taht is being delagated to my (mocked) service
   result = objectUnderTest.doSomthing(parameters)
then: 'I get the result that was provided by my service'
   assert result == someSampleCollection


This proves that it has been 'delegated' correctly which is often all is needed for a unit test.
The content of someSampleCollection is irrelevant, it could even be empty but that just looks really bad and possible could hide a real bug... 
SO I usually populate it with just 1 unique element with as little data as is need to make it unique or what the constructors require. Anything more (Like what you have) distract form what is really being tested and might een mislead
NB : can provide default value as well ,in case property is not defined it can be used.
NB: this might need to become a a parameter later...
you have made changes so should add Bell copyright :_-)

this test it now a generic cpsPath query test; the meaning of the path , the content of the cpsPath variable and the returned data do not matter anymore. 
It is a simple 'delegation' test
no need to test it being called when you your tets depend on mocked behavior. So you can move this to the given: block (mayne as and and:)
